content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: We both need ext.py (same functions: parse() param loop, _make_node signature/call/return). To avoid clobbering, let's go sequentially rather than editing concurrently. My changes are small and isolated: (1) in InternationalizationExtension.parse()'s param while-loop, add an elif branch that recognizes note/created/modified as metadata (not general variables), validates '="literal"' assignment, and fills a local metadata dict; (2) pass metadata into self._make_node(...) call; (3) add a metadata param to _make_node()'s signature; (4) right before the final 'return nodes.Output([node])', set node.trans_metadata = metadata only if metadata is non-empty. I won't touch _install_callables or anything domain-related. Proposal: I go first since my edit is small, send DONE when finished (should be quick), then you make your domain-param edits against the updated file. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start immediately.

I won't touch _install_callables or anything domain-related. Proposal: I go first since my edit is small, send DONE when finished (should be quick), then you make your domain-param edits against the updated file. Sound good? Reply ACCEPT and I'll start immediately.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
your_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Agreed, proceeding independently in separate sandboxes. My hook: new elif branch in the parse() while-loop checking token.value in ('note','created','modified') as metadata keywords (not general variables, not part of the trimmed/notrimmed check), requiring '="string literal"' assignment, accumulated into a local metadata dict. In _make_node, add a metadata param and only set node.trans_metadata = metadata on the returned Output node when the dict is non-empty - additive, no changes to gettext/ngettext/dgettext call construction. Should read as an independent additive block alongside your domain= handling. Starting implementation now.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/jinja2/ext.py
note: Finished trans_metadata implementation: added elif branch in parse()'s param loop for note/created/modified (validates string-literal assignment, raises TemplateAssertionError otherwise), threaded a metadata dict through to _make_node(), and set output_node.trans_metadata only when non-empty. All 830 existing tests pass, babel_extract unaffected, verified with manual render/parse tests. Written to patch.txt. My additions were fully additive to the loop/signature/return - shouldn't conflict with your domain= work as long as we're each adding independent elif/param blocks.
